title: Texas Hold’em Gets Its Prime-Time Moment Back
author: The Press
dek: With the WSOP Main Event returning to ESPN for a three-night final table, poker is trying to turn its biggest tournament back into must-watch TV.
imageAlt: AI-generated photorealistic editorial image of an empty televised poker final table under blue broadcast lights with chips and face-down cards in the foreground.
imageCaptionHtml: AI-generated photorealistic editorial image for The Press showing a televised poker final table. This is not a documentary photograph, official ESPN image, official WSOP image, or real tournament photo.
imageCreditPlain: AI-generated photorealistic editorial image. Not a documentary photograph or official ESPN/WSOP image.
imageAiCaption: This thumbnail is an AI-generated photorealistic editorial image, not a documentary photograph or a real social-media screenshot.
imageCaption: The WSOP Main Event returns to ESPN with a three-night final-table finale in 2026.
excerpt: The 2026 WSOP Main Event gives Texas Hold’em a rare mainstream-TV reset: a July tournament, an August finale, and a chance to make poker feel like a shared sports story again.
